steps.title: Track Mary's reaction
steps.content: Mary works steadily in the garden and becomes more pleased every hour instead of tiring. She experiences the work itself as fascinating play.

steps.title: Reject unsupported motives
steps.content:
• Choice A: The passage never mentions chores or hiding from them.
  • Choice B: It contradicts the statement that Mary becomes more pleased.
  • Choice C: Mary treats the work as play, not merely as preparation for play.

steps.title: State the main idea
steps.content: The passage centers on Mary's satisfaction and absorption while caring for the garden. The answer is D.
